findings.title: The temples of practical magic
findings.html:
3 temples whose catalogued holdings lean unusually hard toward magic-ritual texts — well above the corpus norm. A map of where the working, practical side of the tradition was copied and kept, ranked by how magic-dense each shelf is:

  • Wat Si Mongkhon (Kong) — 30 of 49 manuscripts (61.2%) are magic-ritual
  • Wat Pa Mueat — 30 of 76 manuscripts (39.5%) are magic-ritual
  • Wat Hia — 23 of 91 manuscripts (25.3%) are magic-ritual
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: Where the stars were written down
findings.html:
2 provinces whose surviving corpus leans toward astrology — the reading of time, fate, and the calendar — well above the corpus norm. A geography of where star-lore concentrated on the page:

  • Phayao — 90 of 585 manuscripts (15.4%) are astrology
  • Chiang Rai — 46 of 333 manuscripts (13.8%) are astrology
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The shared vocabulary: words that run from leaf to stall
findings.html:
7 terms are written into the manuscripts and named in today's market — the living thread of the tradition, the same knowledge still changing hands. Ranked by how widely each is carried across both:

  • ผ้ายันต์ — 1 manuscripts · 402 listings
  • ตะกรุด — 7 manuscripts · 367 listings
  • เครื่องราง — 1 manuscripts · 116 listings
  • เมตตา — 21 manuscripts · 80 listings
  • เมตตามหานิยม — 1 manuscripts · 80 listings
  • คงกระพัน — 9 manuscripts · 6 listings
  • ของขลัง — 1 manuscripts · 3 listings
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The minority hands: scripts beyond Tham Lanna
findings.html:
Almost the whole collection is written in Tham Lanna. These are the exceptions — manuscripts in scripts carried across borders and between communities, each a small island of a different writing tradition.

  • thai nithet — 1 manuscripts
  • khom — 2 manuscripts
  • burmese — 13 manuscripts
  • tham lue — 24 manuscripts
  • thai — 72 manuscripts
  • shan — 176 manuscripts
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: Where three tongues share a page
findings.html:
Manuscripts whose language is named as three or more tongues at once — Pali scripture threaded through Shan, Burmese, or Lao. These are the seams of a borderland tradition, where languages met on the same palm leaf.

  • Pali, Shan and Burmese — 63 manuscripts
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The provinces that kept the most
findings.html:
Where the surviving corpus actually lives. Phrae alone accounts for 2133 catalogued manuscripts — a map of where Lanna manuscript culture concentrated and, crucially, where it was preserved.

  • Phrae — 2133 manuscripts
  • Lampang — 1180 manuscripts
  • Chiang Mai — 836 manuscripts
  • Nan — 694 manuscripts
  • Phayao — 585 manuscripts
  • Lamphun — 368 manuscripts
  • Chiang Rai — 333 manuscripts
  • Mae Hong Son — 236 manuscripts
  • Mueang District — 28 manuscripts
  • Sung Men District — 23 manuscripts
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The heavyweights: the longest manuscripts
findings.html:
The twelve longest texts by leaf count, led by one running to 1,046 leaves. Length is its own kind of ambition — these are the epics, chronicles, and encyclopaedias a scribe gave months to.

  • Campa si ton — 1,046 leaves · jataka
  • Suwannacumpu hom — 596 leaves · jataka
  • Utsapalot — 593 leaves · jataka
  • Pannyasacat — 486 leaves · jataka
  • Puttha tamnan — 455 leaves · tamnan chronicle
  • Nisai thutinyavangsamalini — 441 leaves · tamnan chronicle
  • Saliwicai yon hong — 406 leaves · jataka
  • Painyalat — 377 leaves · jataka
  • Siwicai cang phueak — 372 leaves · jataka
  • Latana saeng pue — 349 leaves · jataka
  • Singha pakon — 339 leaves · jataka
  • Sasana mang caen — 331 leaves · tamnan chronicle
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: Already legible to a machine
findings.html:
The manuscripts with the most recognised Thai text — the leading edge of the whole point: making a living tradition readable to a model and not only to a scholar. The frontrunner carries 200,671 characters of transcribed script.

  • Kammatthana Practice Lp Ruesi Ling Dam Vol2 — 200,671 Thai characters across 115 pages · other
  • Kammatthana Practice Lp Ruesi Ling Dam Vol3 — 200,255 Thai characters across 116 pages · other
  • Medicine Manual Of Luang Pu Sukh — 194,306 Thai characters across 170 pages · medicine
  • Kammatthana Practice Lp Ruesi Ling Dam Vol1 — 194,126 Thai characters across 114 pages · other
  • Supreme Maha Mantra Katha — 183,249 Thai characters across 181 pages · magic ritual
  • Making Sacred Charms And Amulets — 171,100 Thai characters across 170 pages · magic ritual
  • Physiognomy Reading Women — 170,505 Thai characters across 204 pages · divination omen
  • Training Mind Power Psychic Vol2 — 166,984 Thai characters across 167 pages · other
  • Mind Stream Key To Saiyasat — 165,296 Thai characters across 175 pages · magic ritual
  • Training Mind Power Psychic Vol1 — 158,722 Thai characters across 167 pages · other
  • Setting Up Ceremonies Ancient Edition — 149,301 Thai characters across 165 pages · other
  • Teaching Meditation And Psychic Power — 142,029 Thai characters across 180 pages · other
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The market's crown jewels
findings.html:
The twelve most valuable objects catalogued from the living market, topped by one listed at 700,000 THB. Where the tradition sets its highest prices is itself a kind of reverence.

  • (Aom) - L-Shaped Belt Decorated with All Genuine Nine-Colored Glass Ge — 700,000 THB
  • พระหลวงพ่อทวดพิมพ์เตารีดหลังหนังสือ ท.ไขว้ ยันต์นูน ปี 2505 — 300,000 THB
  • Jatukamaramthep, Year 2530, Large Print, Crescent Moon, Nakhon Chia Pr — 190,000 THB
  • Talisman Necklace (Fuse, Mega Pavé, Lemurian Quartz, MA18K+DIA+LEM) — 186,700 THB
  • Talisman Necklace SPECIMEN (Songea Aquamarine, DA+GAQU) — 130,100 THB
  • Talisman Necklace SPECIMEN (Aquamarine, KA+AQU) — 109,800 THB
  • Nang Kwak Mahanont Behind R.5. Krub Wang Wat Phra Kaew 2461. the Side — 99,999 THB
  • หุ่นพยนต์พ่อฤทธิ์ ปี54 แท้100% — 99,999 THB
  • Talisman Cuboid Necklace (Zambian Topaz, DA+TOZ) — 94,100 THB
  • Talisman Cuboid Necklace (Tanzanite, KA+TAN) — 75,700 THB
  • หินทิเบตเขี้ยวเสือและเกลียวคลื่น (Tiger Tooth and Wave dZi Bead) — 75,000 THB
  • Talisman Necklace (Brace-Held, Imperial Topaz, PA+ITOP) — 68,500 THB
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The deepest roots in the collection
findings.html:
The ten earliest-dated manuscripts, reaching back to 1471 CE — the far anchor of the single continuous timeline that runs all the way to today's amulet stalls.

  • Tiṁsa nipāta — 1471 CE · jataka
  • Milintapanha — 1495 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Pali yamaka — 1497 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Aṭṭhakathā mullapaṇṇāsa majjhimanikāya — 1501 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Aṭṭha nipāta — 1503 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Nam sap sammohawinotani — 1505 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Anuṭīkā paṭṭhāna — 1511 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Tingsa nibat — 1514 CE · jataka
  • Petawatthu — 1514 CE · buddhist canonical
  • Paṇṇāsa nipāta — 1514 CE · jataka
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: Words the market speaks that the manuscripts never wrote
findings.html:
These terms are common in the living market yet appear in no catalogued manuscript — proof that the tradition is not a closed archive but a language still adding words.

  • thai amulet — 555 listings
  • buddha amulet — 522 listings
  • talisman — 498 listings
  • amulet — 488 listings
  • สีผึ้งมหาเสน่ห์ — 456 listings
  • ผงพุทธคุณ — 450 listings
  • พระพิมพ์ — 415 listings
  • ท้าวเวสสุวรรณ — 412 listings
  • นางกวัก — 412 listings
  • พญาเต่าเรือน — 412 listings
  • กุมารทอง — 408 listings
  • พระสมเด็จ — 407 listings
  • จตุคาม — 405 listings
  • หลวงปู่ทวด — 405 listings
  • ชานหมาก — 403 listings

findings.title: Words the manuscripts keep that the market has let go
findings.html:
Written into many manuscripts yet named in no current listing — the part of the tradition that lives on the page but no longer on the stall.

  • ยา — 29 manuscripts
  • พระ — 28 manuscripts
  • นาค — 25 manuscripts
  • สัก — 25 manuscripts
  • อาจารย์ — 24 manuscripts
  • ครู — 23 manuscripts
  • ผี — 22 manuscripts
  • เทพ — 20 manuscripts
  • มนต์ — 19 manuscripts
  • คาถา — 18 manuscripts
  • น้ำมัน — 15 manuscripts
  • ยันต์ — 12 manuscripts
  • สมาธิ — 12 manuscripts
  • เสน่ห์ — 11 manuscripts
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: Where the drawn tradition is thickest
findings.html:
Manuscripts carrying the most diagram pages — the yantra, cosmological charts, and talismanic figures that are drawn, not written. The visual heart of the collection, and a good place to just look.

  • Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing — 54 diagram pages · magic ritual
  • Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth — 39 diagram pages · other
  • Making Sacred Charms And Amulets — 23 diagram pages · magic ritual
  • Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage — 6 diagram pages · magic ritual
  • Maha Yant Manual — 3 diagram pages · magic ritual
findings.author: curiosity-bot

findings.title: The 56 khoi manuscripts
findings.html: Only 56 manuscripts in the collection are written on khoi; the overwhelming majority are palm-leaf.
findings.author: curiosity-bot
